_comment: Generative conformance scenarios. Each one drives `claude -p` to emit a controlled session, then the harness validates the resulting JSONL against the contract. `trigger`=auto runs headless; `trigger`=manual must be produced by a human (documented in README). `cost`=high scenarios are skipped unless --all is passed. `checks` drive run.sh: schema (Go Pass0+Pass3 drift), usage_match (result-block usage == in-file usage), surface (named event/block types must appear), agent_tokens (subagent-sum vs totalTokens vs chars/4), iterations_populated (usage.iterations non-empty).

scenarios.surface: user, assistant, message.usage, ai-title, attachment, queue-operation, last-prompt
scenarios.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: PONG

scenarios.surface: tool_use, tool_result content blocks, Bash toolUseResult shape
scenarios.prompt: Run this exact command with the Bash tool and report nothing else: echo conformance-probe
Create a file named probe.txt containing the line 'alpha' using the Write tool, then use the Edit tool to change 'alpha' to 'beta'. Report nothing else.
Use the Bash tool to run `echo one`, then use the Bash tool again to run `echo two`, then report DONE.

Use the Agent tool with subagent_type general-purpose to perform this task: 'Run the Bash command `echo from-subagent`, then write a 3-sentence summary of what you observed.' Then relay the subagent's summary.
MANUAL: start an interactive `claude` session in the scratch dir, send one prompt, detach/pause, then resume it. The reattach emits system/away_summary. See README.

scenarios.surface: permission-mode and mode events on live transitions
scenarios.prompt: MANUAL: in an interactive `claude` session, toggle permission mode (shift-tab) and any output/fast mode. Each toggle emits a permission-mode and/or mode event. See README.
